title: Hattie's Grand Playhouse Construction
summary: Hattie the mouse hears a strange digging sound and discovers Jengia the Digger building a brand new playhouse in her backyard. The construction project proceeds through several careful steps, including the addition of a bench, a door, walls, and colorful curtains. When the group reaches the final challenge of placing the heavy roof, they call upon Lily, the tallest mouse in the world, to help them finish the job and complete Hattie's new home.

highlightQuote.text: Maybe you can ask the tallest in world mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What'd you say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You need to turn off the lamp.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. There's the basket with the stars. I'll turn off the lamp. Okay, it's time for stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, let's have a story about Hattie the mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Oh, but I didn't set the blue timer. So we'll just have to make sure it's a fairly short story. Let's see.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: About Hattie... the house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Hattie the mouse was sitting in her house and she heard an interesting sound from outside. It sounded like this: Gsh-gsh, gsh-gsh, gsh-gsh. And she thought to herself, "I wonder what that sound is." What did she think it was?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A digger!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Gsh-gsh, gsh-gsh. It got even louder. And she thought to herself, "A digger outside my house? I wonder what it's doing there." What did she think it might be doing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's making a house! It's making you a playhouse!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a new playhouse for Hattie the mouse. And so Hattie opened the door to her backyard and what did she see back there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A digger making a new playhouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What was the first step to make the new playhouse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Add the bench.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Add the bench. And so Hattie said, "Oh, I see you're working on my new playhouse, digger." And what did the digger say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm working and still working on this.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you're still working on it. What's your name, digger?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Jengia.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Jengia? Oh, well I'm Hattie, and thanks for building me a playhouse. Now I know that there's a lot of steps in building a playhouse, but I think the first one is adding a bench. Are you adding the bench, digger?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Gsh-gsh, gsh-gsh. And he picked up the bench parts and put them in place and then he used his screwdriver—Bzzzt!—to screw the parts together. And then the bench was all done and Hattie said, "Now it's time for the next step. That was step one, add a bench. What's step two?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Add the door.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Add the door. What's step three going to be after that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's going to be add the walls.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Add the walls. And then step four?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's going to be the windows.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what's step five?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Add the curtains.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then step six? Maybe the roof?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, then the roof.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, Jengia, let's get to work. So the next step is the door. Zzzp! And he added the door. Okay, what was the next step? Step three was the walls, I think. Zzzp! Okay, we added the walls. But now we need to do the next step, which was the windows. Clink, clink! Okay, we added the windows. And the step after that was the curtains. Um, Hattie, can you help me decide what kind of curtains to get? What should they look like?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They are round and curly and stripy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, round, curly, and stripy. And what color should the curtains be?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Red, blue, orange, yellow, green.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Whoa, that's a lot of colors. Okay, here's your curtains. Zip! I'll put them in the windows. Bop, bop, bop! Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Next... a roof!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Now the last step is putting the roof on. This is a big step because the roof is heavy. Will you help me lift it, Hattie Mouse? And then the digger and Hattie tried to lift the roof... Gsh, gsh... Oh no, it's too heavy. What can we do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe you can ask the tallest in world mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The tallest in the world mouse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, it's Lily.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Lily is the tallest mouse in the world, said Jengia?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, can you give her a call? And Hattie Mouse said, "Okay. Beep-boop-beep. Ring, ring, ring! Excuse me, is Lily the tallest mouse in the world there?" And did she answer?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She did answer.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She said, "Hello Hattie, nice to hear from you. What's up?" And what did Hattie the mouse say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The roof is too heavy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh, maybe I can help with that," said Lily Mouse. "I'll be right over." And so Lily the mouse, who was the tallest mouse in the whole world, came over and now all three of them set out to lift the roof. Lily and Hattie and the digger. And they said, on the count of three. Will you count with me? Okay, let's count together. One...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Two, three, lift!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Gsh-gsh-gsh! And they lifted the roof which was so heavy and then—Pop!—they popped it right onto the house and then screwed it on. Bzzzt! And now the house was all done and Hattie the mouse said, "Thank you so much for building me a playhouse in my backyard." And that's the end of our story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And I want the basket...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's do something about shadows.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, some shadow puppets? Chomp, chomp, chomp. Here, if you come over here we'll be able to see the shadows of your fingers. Wow, so cool. Stick out your fingers. Oh, there they are. Wow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's a butterfly. It's so cool.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's a bird.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's a bird. I see it. Wow. So fun to do shadow puppets. Okay, do you need a diaper change? You just had one before lunch, didn't you? But we can change it again if you need it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No diaper change? Okay.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Jengia
characters.description: A helpful digger who builds a multi-step playhouse for Hattie.

characters.name: Lily
characters.description: The world’s tallest mouse is a guardian scholar of monumental power and studious grace, serving as the living pillar of a communal house where he hoists the massive roof with peerless strength. Between shifts at the Pepperoni Street tape store and classes at the local school, this friend of Mistress Mouse finds quiet joy building with Legos or coloring in the baby lounge alongside his many brothers. Adorned in his signature shell necklace and a fresh bespoke hat from Haddy, he remains a gentle soul whose immense physical duty is matched only by his dedicated, playful heart.

places.name: The Backyard
places.description: The site behind Hattie's house where the new playhouse is constructed.
